title: Voter Integrity Protection Act
plain_summary: This bill makes new punishments for people who are not US citizens but vote in federal elections anyway. If someone is in the country illegally and votes in a federal election, they can now be charged with a serious crime called an aggravated felony. People who do this can also be deported from the United States. The bill is meant to stop non-citizens from voting in elections for Congress and President.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Voter Integrity Protection Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Unlawful Voting
sections.text: This section changes existing immigration laws to add new penalties for voting illegally. First, it adds a new type of serious crime called an aggravated felony. This crime happens when someone who is in the United States illegally votes in a federal election. Federal elections include voting for President, Congress members, or Senators. Second, it makes illegal voting a reason to remove someone from the country. Any person who is in the United States illegally and knowingly votes in a federal election can be deported. The law refers to a specific criminal code that already makes it illegal for non-citizens to vote in federal elections.
